ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Please select the option that best describes the worst severity of each symptom during the past 24 hours: Bloating (feeling like you need to loosen your clothes)
Please select the option that best describes the worst severity of each symptom during the past 24 hours: Nausea (feeling sick to your stomach as if you were going to vomit or throw up)
Please select the option that best describes the worst severity of each symptom during the past 24 hours: Not able to finish a normal-sized meal (for a healthy person)
Please select the option that best describes the worst severity of each symptom during the past 24 hours: Feeling excessively full after meals
Please select the option that best describes the worst severity of each symptom during the past 24 hours: Upper abdominal pain (above the navel)
During the past 24 hours, how many episodes of vomiting did you have?
In thinking about the symptoms above, what was the overall severity of your symptoms today?

Notes: Average of gcsi2, gcsi3, gcsi4, gcsi5, gcsi6 (capped)
e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2; Morning, At Bedtime
